title: Acid Nucleic Extraction from Rice Dried Leaves
author: Martine Bangratz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcntiven

steps.title: Grind the dried leaves with the...
steps.instruction: Grind the dried leaves with the Qiagen TissueLyser II until obtaining a fine powder

steps.title: Add 1 ml CTAB extraction buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml CTAB extraction buffer (see 'before start' for the buffer content) and homogenize by vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate at 65°C for 30 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65°C for 30 min. Periodically, mix gently the tubes during the incubation. 65 °C 00:30:00 10000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant (650µl) to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant (650µl) to a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. Add equal volume of chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (24:1)

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant (500µl) in a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant (500µl) in a new 1.5 ml tube containing 350 µl of isopropanol (pre-chilled at -20°C). Mix gently -20 °C 00:30:00 15000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant . Wash the pellet with 70% ethanol. 15000 x g, °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Remove the ethanol and air-dried the pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the ethanol and air-dried the pellet.

steps.title: Dissolve the pellet in 50 µl...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the pellet in 50 µl sterile water or TE buffer and conserve the DNA at -20°C.
